title: Testing, Static Quality, Performance, and Release · Retrieval Circuit
summary: Harden the service-data client with behavioral tests, deterministic fixtures, performance evidence, and a clean release. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a tested and release-ready JavaScript application after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design tests around observable behavior, changed inputs, boundaries, and failures rather than implementation lines.
Use fakes, stubs, spies, and controlled clocks only at owned boundaries without mocking away the behavior under test.
Await asynchronous outcomes, control timers and network fixtures, and fail on unhandled work deterministically.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.javascript:
// javascript-basics cumulative stakeholder program.
// Add one runnable behavior, assertion, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Test Behavior evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Test Behavior assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: A release candidate passes the familiar 3-record fixture but fails an empty list and a duplicate identifier. Reproduce both, add behavior and accessibility regressions, and reject release until the clean build agrees. Competency probe: Design tests around observable behavior, changed inputs, boundaries, and failures rather than implementation lines.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Test Behavior has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Test Behavior precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: A release candidate passes the familiar 3-record fixture but fails an empty list and a duplicate identifier. Reproduce both, add behavior and accessibility regressions, and reject release until the clean build agrees. Competency probe: Design tests around observable behavior, changed inputs, boundaries, and failures rather than implementation lines.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: High line coverage proves the important behavior is correct.

options.text: Declare inputs, observable output, ownership, and the runtime behavior being tested for the review Test Behavior case
Trace values, bindings, receivers, mutations, and queued work before editing code for the review Test Behavior case
Implement the smallest behavior and retain an assertion over the actual result for the review Test Behavior case
Run boundary and changed-event cases, inspect failures, and remove accidental coupling for the review Test Behavior case

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write the review evidence note for Release Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A release candidate passes the familiar 6-record fixture but fails an empty list and a duplicate identifier. Reproduce both, add behavior and accessibility regressions, and reject release until the clean build agrees. Competency probe: Produce a clean-environment build, supported-runtime record, accessibility checks, rollback plan, and stakeholder acceptance evidence.
steps.why: Retrieving Release Evidence through the concrete a tested and release-ready JavaScript application case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Produce a clean-environment build, supported-runtime record, accessibility checks, rollback plan, and stakeholder acceptance evidence.
